title: Dashboard
summary: The Dashboard is the operations hub for Airfield Managers — surfacing today's inspection status, last-completed check, module shortcut tiles, inline shift checklist, and the Out-of-Office / Close-Airfield toggles that broadcast a full-screen overlay to every connected user.
concept:
The Dashboard (/dashboard) is a read-mostly KPI surface backed entirely by lib/dashboard-context.tsx, which holds the live airfield_status row in React state and keeps it current via a Supabase Realtime channel (postgres_changes on UPDATE events filtered to base_id). A 30-second visibility-gated polling fallback covers the rare case where Realtime is unavailable.

Key surfaces

  • Inspection status strip — two clickable tiles (Airfield / Lighting) that show today's inspection state (Not Started / In Progress / Complete) using a 0600 local-time reset scoped to the installation's timezone.
  • Module shortcut tiles — an auto-fill grid linking to Checks, Discrepancies, Contractors, Shift Checklist, QRCs, SCN, PPR, and BASH. Each tile is gated by isModuleEnabled() so disabled modules never appear.
  • Out-of-Office toggle — requires airfield_status:write. On activation, writes afm_out_of_office: true and afm_ooo_message to the airfield_status row, then logs a manual activity-log entry. The Airfield Status page shows a full-screen overlay to all users via Realtime.
  • Close-Airfield toggle — also requires airfield_status:write. On activation it also clears all runway_statuses, RSC, RCR, and BWC so the next opening check starts from a blank slate. Clearing is an intentional design choice — historical Events Log rows are unaffected.
  • Inline dialogs — Personnel on Airfield (calls createContractor), Shift Checklist (three-state toggle with progress bar, 0600 reset, per-item N/A support), and QRCs (template picker + active-exec list).

Key tables / columns

TableRelevant columns
airfield_statusafm_out_of_office, afm_ooo_message, afm_closed, afm_closed_message, runway_statuses (JSONB), rsc_condition, rcr_*, bwc_value
airfield_checkscheck_type, completed_at — queried for the last-check pill
inspectionsinspection_type, inspection_date, status — queried for the inspection strip
shift_checklist_items / shift_checklist_responsesinline checklist dialog

The local-update guard in DashboardProvider.refreshStatus skips a polling refresh if a local mutation happened within the last 15 seconds (lastLocalUpdate.current), preventing optimistic-update races where the poll would overwrite what the user just typed.

code.note: The channel filter `base_id=eq.${installationId}` ensures each base only receives its own status updates.

annotations.note: Supabase Realtime channel: listens for UPDATE events on airfield_status; unpacks every field (advisories, runways, ARFF, RSC/RCR/BWC, OOO, closed) into local state so all consumers re-render.

code.caption: setAfmClosed — clears runway/RSC/RCR/BWC on close to ensure a clean opening check
code.from: // Activate / deactivate "closed for the day". On activate, clear per-runway
code.note: Only the close path clears runway/condition fields; the deactivate path leaves them blank so operators set fresh values during the opening check.

annotations.note: On `active=true` each runway in runwayLabels is reset to status='open' with null remarks, then all RSC/RCR/BWC fields are nulled in the DB patch.

code.caption: Inspection status strip and last-check pill — 0600L reset and Realtime refresh
code.note: The 0600L reset works by computing the local date in the installation's timezone; if the local hour is before 6, the previous calendar day is used.

annotations.note: loadLastCheck: queries airfield_checks ordered by completed_at DESC limit 1, formats with CHECK_TYPE_CONFIG label.
Today's inspection derivation: converts UTC now to installation timezone, rolls back one day when hour < 6, then matches inspections by inspection_date string.
Realtime INSERT listener on airfield_checks: triggers loadLastCheck so the last-check pill updates the moment a new check is filed by any user.

code.caption: Out-of-Office activation — writes to airfield_status and logs an activity entry
code.note: The activity-log entry ('AMOPS out of office, Command Post notified') is a manual entry distinct from the status write — both happen inside the click handler.

annotations.note: setAfmOutOfOffice + logManualEntry are called sequentially; the Realtime channel propagates the status change to all tabs.

fits:
The Dashboard does not write to the activity log automatically — all activity-log entries from Dashboard actions are manual entries written by logManualEntry() in lib/supabase/activity.ts, ensuring the Events Log records the operator's intent text rather than a raw field change.

The DashboardProvider is mounted above the entire authenticated layout in app/(app)/layout.tsx, so every authenticated page can call useDashboard() to read runway status, ARFF category, RSC, or OOO state — for example, the Airfield Status page (/) renders the OOO overlay using this context. The Airfield Checks module uses the last-check Realtime channel on the Dashboard to update the header pill without a full page reload. The Shift Checklist dialog reuses the same shift_checklist_items / responses tables as the dedicated /shift-checklist page, with a 0600L reset scoped to currentInstallation.timezone. Personnel on Airfield entries created from the Dashboard tile land in the same contractors table as /contractors.

quiz.q: When `setAfmClosed(true, message)` is called, which fields are explicitly cleared in the DB update patch?
choices: Only afm_closed and afm_closed_message
runway_statuses, runway_status, rsc_condition, rsc_updated_at, all rcr_* columns, bwc_value, bwc_updated_at — in addition to the closed flag/message
All fields in airfield_status are reset to null
Only RSC and BWC are cleared; runway statuses are preserved
quiz.explain: The setAfmClosed callback builds a patch that includes runway_statuses (each set to status='open' with null remarks), runway_status, rsc_condition, rsc_updated_at, rcr_touchdown, rcr_midpoint, rcr_rollout, rcr_condition, rcr_updated_at, bwc_value, and bwc_updated_at, plus the closed flag and message. This ensures a clean slate for the next opening check.

quiz.q: Why does `refreshStatus` in DashboardProvider skip the DB fetch when `Date.now() - lastLocalUpdate.current < 15000`?
choices: To reduce Supabase read quota usage for cost savings
To prevent a polling refresh from overwriting optimistic state right after a local mutation
Because Realtime always fires within 15 seconds and polling is redundant
To throttle writes to the airfield_status table
quiz.explain: When an Airfield Manager changes runway status or RSC, the context immediately applies the new value optimistically. A polling refresh arriving within the next 15 seconds could overwrite that state with the pre-mutation DB value, creating a visible flicker. The guard short-circuits the poll during that window.

quiz.q: Which permission key gates both the Out-of-Office toggle and the Close-Airfield toggle on the Dashboard?
choices: PERM.DASHBOARD_WRITE
PERM.AIRFIELD_STATUS_WRITE
PERM.BASE_SETUP_WRITE
PERM.REPORTS_READ
quiz.explain: Line 35 of dashboard/page.tsx: `const canToggleOoo = has(PERM.AIRFIELD_STATUS_WRITE)`. Both OOO and Close-Airfield buttons check this same flag, because both operations write the airfield_status row.

title: Discrepancy Tracking
summary: The discrepancy module is the primary airfield defect-tracking workflow — covering creation with auto-assigned display IDs, a five-state workflow rail between AMOPS and CES, a column-scoped SECURITY DEFINER RPC that lets CES personnel write only their permitted fields, and a full audit trail in the status_updates table.
concept:
Discrepancies (/discrepancies) implement the DAFMAN 13-204 defect-reporting workflow. When an Airfield Manager identifies a hazard, they open a new discrepancy at /discrepancies/new. The system assigns a display_id in the format D-YYYY-XXXX (where XXXX is a 4-char base-36 timestamp fragment) and sets status = 'open' with an initial current_status of submitted_to_afm or submitted_to_ces depending on the type-to-shop mapping.

Two status dimensions

  • status — the lifecycle status: open, completed, cancelled. Written only by Airfield Manager-role users via updateDiscrepancyStatus().
  • current_status — the workflow position within an open discrepancy. Five values in CURRENT_STATUS_OPTIONS: submitted_to_afm, submitted_to_ces, awaiting_action_by_ces, waiting_for_project, work_completed_awaiting_verification.

CES write path vs. full write path

CES personnel have a restricted role that cannot satisfy the discrepancies:write RLS policy. They must use the ces_update_discrepancy SECURITY DEFINER RPC, which is column-scoped: it only allows updating current_status (restricted to the three CES-side values), resolution_notes, and an optional note that lands in status_updates. Airfield Managers use the direct updateDiscrepancy() function, which goes through the normal RLS write path. The cesUpdateDiscrepancy() client wrapper in lib/supabase/discrepancies.ts calls this RPC.

KPI counters and the list page

The list page (/discrepancies/page.tsx) defines three KPI buckets: AFM (submitted_to_afm), CES (submitted_to_ces | awaiting_action_by_ces | waiting_for_project), and AMOPS (work_completed_awaiting_verification). Clicking a KPI chip sets a filter that narrows the list. The fetchPendingVerificationCount() function powers the sidebar badge count for work completed awaiting verification.

No list-level Realtime

The discrepancy list is not a Realtime subscription. It loads on mount and on manual refresh. Only the sidebar badge count uses a custom-event bridge from mutation sites to update without a full poll. The detail page (/discrepancies/[id]) also re-fetches on demand rather than subscribing.

Key tables

TablePurpose
discrepanciesOne row per defect; display_id, status, current_status, location, photos count, linked NAVAID FK
status_updatesAppend-only audit log; old_status/new_status for lifecycle transitions, notes for current_status changes and manual notes
photosPer-discrepancy photos keyed by discrepancy_id FK; storage at discrepancy-photos/{id}/{ts}.ext in the photos bucket

CES Work Orders (/ces)

The /ces page is the Airfield Manager role's dedicated landing page for CES users. It filters discrepancies to only those with a CES-side current_status that are still status='open' and have an assigned_shop. Shop tabs are driven by the per-base ceShops list from useInstallation(). Status transitions from this page call cesUpdateDiscrepancy() through StatusUpdateModal.

code.caption: createDiscrepancy — display_id generation, default current_status, and photo_count init
code.note: The display_id uses base-36 encoding of the last 4 characters of the epoch timestamp — not a sequential counter, so there are no gaps or collisions across concurrent inserts.

annotations.note: display_id: D-{year}-{ts}. `ts = now.getTime().toString(36).slice(-4).toUpperCase()` — base-36 last-4 of epoch ms.
Default current_status is submitted_to_afm unless the caller overrides it (e.g. when the type-to-shop map resolves a CES shop, the new-discrepancy page sets submitted_to_ces).
work_order_number is initialized to 'Pending' — CES will update it when a real WO is assigned.

code.caption: cesUpdateDiscrepancy — SECURITY DEFINER RPC for column-scoped CES writes
code.from:
/**
 * CES write path — calls the SECURITY DEFINER RPC so `ces` users
code.to:
return { data: data as DiscrepancyRow | null, error: null }
}
code.note: The RPC `ces_update_discrepancy` lives in the DB and enforces its own column-scope guard; the client never tries a direct UPDATE on discrepancies for the CES role.

annotations.note: Comment explains exactly which current_status values the RPC permits (awaiting_action_by_ces, waiting_for_project, work_completed_awaiting_verification) — this is enforced in the DB function, not here.
The RPC call passes p_id, p_current_status, p_resolution_notes, p_note. The DB function writes status_updates atomically so the audit row and the discrepancy update are in the same transaction.

code.caption: fetchPendingVerificationCount — sidebar badge for work awaiting AMOPS verification
code.from: // Count of discrepancies that have been marked work-complete by CES and
code.note: Filters both status='open' AND current_status='work_completed_awaiting_verification' — without the status guard, closed rows with a stale current_status would inflate the badge.

annotations.note: Uses head:true count query (no rows fetched) for minimal payload. Returns 0 on Supabase misconfiguration.

code.caption: KPI bucket definitions and current_status color map on the list page
code.note: KPI_DEFS defines which current_status enum values each bucket covers. The CES bucket spans three values because a discrepancy is 'with CES' whether it is newly submitted, actively being worked, or parked for a project.

annotations.note: CURRENT_STATUS_COLORS: routes each of the five enum values to a theme token (inwork/orange/warning/purple/success) for consistent left-rail coloring.

code.caption: DISCREPANCY_TYPES and CURRENT_STATUS_OPTIONS — the canonical enum definitions
code.note: DISCREPANCY_TYPES drives the type picker on new/edit forms, the color-coded icons in list and map views, and the defaultShop fuzzy-match on auto-assignment.

fits:
The discrepancy module integrates deeply with several other modules:

  • Visual NAVAIDs (/infrastructure): Reporting a NAVAID outage at /infrastructure auto-creates a linked discrepancy and sets the feature's status to inoperative. Creating a discrepancy at /discrepancies/new with a linked feature goes the other direction — it calls bulkUpdateStatus and createOutageEvent to mark the feature inoperative and log the outage.
  • Activity log: Status transitions (via updateDiscrepancyStatus and cesUpdateDiscrepancy) write to status_updates, not to activity_log directly. The Events Log page reads status_updates as part of its rollup.
  • Photos: Photos are stored in the photos bucket under path discrepancy-photos/{id}/{ts}.ext. RLS on the bucket verifies the discrepancy_id UUID in the path plus photos:write permission.
  • SMS Hazard promotion (civilian mode only): The detail page can promote a discrepancy to an SMS hazard via createHazard(), gated on isCivilian(currentInstallation) && has(PERM.SMS_WRITE).
  • Sidebar badge: fetchPendingVerificationCount feeds the AMOPS badge. The badge refreshes via a custom-event bridge triggered by mutation sites and a 60-second polling fallback, not Realtime.
  • PDF / Email export: Both the list and detail pages can generate a discrepancy PDF (via lib/discrepancy-pdf.ts) and email it through the /api/send-pdf-email route handler (Resend).

quiz.q: Why must CES personnel call `cesUpdateDiscrepancy()` (the SECURITY DEFINER RPC) rather than `updateDiscrepancy()` directly?
choices: updateDiscrepancy() is too slow for CES use cases

CES users lack the discrepancies:write RLS permission that updateDiscrepancy() requires, so a direct UPDATE is blocked by RLS; the SECURITY DEFINER RPC runs with elevated privileges scoped to only the fields CES is allowed to touch
cesUpdateDiscrepancy() also sends a notification email to the Airfield Manager
updateDiscrepancy() does not write a status_updates audit row
quiz.explain: The comment in discrepancies.ts explains it directly: 'ces users (who are blocked by the RLS user_can_write gate) can update the narrow set of fields the CES workflow allows'. The SECURITY DEFINER function bypasses the RLS check but enforces its own column-scope guard in the DB function body, allowing only current_status, resolution_notes, and note.

quiz.q: A discrepancy is `status='completed'` but its `current_status` is still `work_completed_awaiting_verification`. Does `fetchPendingVerificationCount` count it in the sidebar badge?
choices: Yes — current_status is the only filter
No — the query also filters status='open', so completed rows are excluded even if current_status was never reset
It depends on the base configuration
Yes, but only if assigned_shop is non-null
quiz.explain: fetchPendingVerificationCount explicitly adds `.eq('status', 'open')` before the `.eq('current_status', 'work_completed_awaiting_verification')` filter. The comment in the source says 'Filters status='open' so closed/cancelled rows whose current_status was never re-cleared don't pollute the badge.'

quiz.q: When a new discrepancy is created and a Visual NAVAID feature is selected, what three side-effects happen after `createDiscrepancy()` succeeds?
choices: An email is sent, a NOTAM is created, and the feature is marked inoperative
bulkUpdateStatus() marks selected features 'inoperative', createOutageEvent() logs an outage event per feature, and uploadDiscrepancyPhoto() uploads any attached photos
The feature is marked inoperative, a daily review record is created, and the activity log is updated
A discrepancy PDF is auto-generated, the feature status is updated, and a status_updates row is inserted
quiz.explain: After createDiscrepancy() succeeds, /discrepancies/new/page.tsx calls bulkUpdateStatus(selectedFeatureIds, 'inoperative') to mark each linked feature as inoperative, then loops over selectedFeatureIds calling createOutageEvent() for each one (with event_type='reported'). Finally it loops over photos calling uploadDiscrepancyPhoto(). All three happen client-side in sequence after the DB insert.
